goal: Build a RAG-powered AI insight agent for the Weekly Commit application.   ## Context   This is a Spring Boot + React monorepo for weekly commitment planning. The backend is at `backend/`, frontend at `frontend/`. The AI layer already has:   - `AiProvider` interface + `AiProviderRegistry` (strategy pattern with graceful degradation)   - `OpenRouterAiProvider` (production LLM calls via OpenRouter)   - `AiSuggestionService` (stores suggestions + feedback in `ai_suggestion` / `ai_feedback` tables)   - `AiController` with 7 endpoints including `POST /api/ai/feedback`   - Existing services: CommitDraftAssist, CommitLint, RcdoSuggest, RiskDetection, ReconcileAssist, ManagerAiSummary   API keys are in `.env` (gitignored): `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`, `PINECONE_API_KEY`. Config is in `backend/src/main/resources/application.yml` under `ai.openrouter.*` and `ai.pinecone.*`.   ## What to build   ### 1. Pinecone integration (backend)   Create `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/`:   - `PineconeClient.java` — HTTP client to Pinecone REST API (upsert, query, delete vectors). Use `java.net.http.HttpClient`. Config from `ai.pinecone.api-key`, `ai.pinecone.index-name`. The index needs to be created via the Pinecone API on first use if it doesn't exist (dimension: 1536, metric: dotproduct, spec: serverless in us-east-1).   - `EmbeddingService.java` — Calls OpenRouter embedding endpoint (`POST /api/v1/embeddings` with model `openai/text-embedding-3-small`) to get 1536-dim vectors. Returns `float[]`. Config from `ai.openrouter.api-key`, `ai.embedding.model`.   - `ChunkBuilder.java` — Builds text chunks + Pinecone metadata from database entities. One chunk per entity:     - `commit`: "{title}" — {chessPiece}, {estimatePoints}pts. Description: {description}. Success criteria: {successCriteria}. Outcome: {outcome}. Notes: {outcomeNotes}     - `scope_change`: Scope change on "{commitTitle}": {category} — reason: {reason}     - `carry_forward`: Carry-forward: "{commitTitle}" — reason: {reason}. Notes: {reasonNotes}. Streak: {streak}     - `manager_comment`: Manager comment on plan {weekStartDate}: "{content}"     - `ticket`: [{key}] "{title}" — {status}, {priority}. Description: {description}     - `plan_summary`: Week of {weekStartDate}: {commitCount} commits, {totalPoints}pts, {state}     Metadata per chunk: `userId`, `teamId`, `planId`, `weekStartDate`, `entityType`, `entityId`   - `SemanticIndexService.java` — Orchestrates write-time indexing. When entities are created/updated, builds chunk → embeds → upserts to Pinecone. Has a `@Scheduled` daily sweep to re-index anything that failed. Uses `@Async` for non-blocking writes. Namespace in Pinecone = org ID.   - `SemanticQueryService.java` — The query pipeline:     1. Intent classification: Send question to OpenRouter LLM with structured output to extract `{ intent, userFilter, timeRange, entityTypes, keywords }`     2. Embed the question via EmbeddingService     3. Hybrid query to Pinecone (dense vector + sparse keyword matching via metadata filters derived from intent)     4. Take top 5 results     5. Pack retrieved chunks + original question into LLM context     6. Generate structured answer: `{ answer, sources: [{entityType, entityId, weekStartDate, snippet}], confidence }`     7. Store in `ai_suggestion` table with `suggestion_type = 'RAG_QUERY'`   - `InsightGenerationService.java` — Proactive insight agent:     1. Triggered by a scheduled job (daily at 08:00 UTC) or on-demand     2. For each team: gathers current week data (plans, compliance, RCDO coverage, carry-forward chains, scope changes)     3. Queries Pinecone for relevant historical context ("patterns in this team's last 4 weeks")     4. Sends to LLM with system prompt: "Generate 3-5 actionable management insights. Each must cite specific plans/commits/tickets."     5. Stores each insight as an `ai_suggestion` with `suggestion_type = 'TEAM_INSIGHT'` or `'PERSONAL_INSIGHT'`     6. Insights have structured payload: `{ insightText, severity, sourceEntityIds, actionSuggestion }`   ### 2. New controller endpoints   Add to `AiController.java`:   - `POST /api/ai/query` — On-demand RAG query. Request: `{ question, teamId?, userId? }`. Response: `{ answer, sources, confidence, suggestionId }`. Uses `SemanticQueryService`.   - `GET /api/teams/{id}/week/{weekStart}/ai-insights` — Returns pre-computed insight cards for a team+week. Reads from `ai_suggestion` where `suggestion_type = 'TEAM_INSIGHT'`.   - `GET /api/plans/{id}/ai-insights` — Returns pre-computed personal insight cards for a plan. Reads from `ai_suggestion` where `suggestion_type = 'PERSONAL_INSIGHT'`.   ### 3. Event-driven indexing   Add write-time indexing hooks. When these entities are created or updated, call `SemanticIndexService.indexEntity()` asynchronously:   - `WeeklyCommit` (on create, update, outcome set)   - `ScopeChangeEvent` (on create)   - `CarryForwardLink` (on create)   - `ManagerComment` (on create)   - `WorkItem` (on create, update, status transition)   - `WeeklyPlan` (on lock, reconcile submit)   The simplest approach: add calls at the end of the existing service methods (CommitService, ScopeChangeService, CarryForwardService, etc.) rather than using Spring Events. The `@Async` annotation on `SemanticIndexService.indexEntity()` ensures the write path isn't blocked.   ### 4. Frontend components   Create `frontend/src/components/ai/`:   - `InsightPanel.tsx` — Fetches and displays pre-computed insight cards. Used on both My Week (`GET /api/plans/{id}/ai-insights`) and Team Week (`GET /api/teams/{id}/week/{weekStart}/ai-insights`). Each card shows: insight text, severity indicator, source links (clickable → navigate to plan/ticket), thumbs up/down via existing `AiFeedbackButtons` component.   - `SemanticSearchInput.tsx` — Search input that calls `POST /api/ai/query`. Shows a loading state while the query runs (~2s). Renders the answer in a `QueryAnswerCard` below the input.   - `QueryAnswerCard.tsx` — Displays: answer text, source citations with entity type badges and clickable links, confidence indicator, `AiFeedbackButtons`.   Wire `InsightPanel` into:   - `frontend/src/routes/MyWeek.tsx` — below the plan header, above the commit list   - `frontend/src/routes/TeamWeek.tsx` — as a collapsible section above the tab bar   Wire `SemanticSearchInput` into:   - `frontend/src/routes/TeamWeek.tsx` — in the page header area   ### 5. API client + hooks   Create `frontend/src/api/ragApi.ts` with functions for the 3 new endpoints.   Create `frontend/src/api/ragHooks.ts` with `useTeamInsights(teamId, weekStart)`, `usePlanInsights(planId)`, and a `useSemanticQuery()` mutation hook.   ### 6. Tests   Backend:   - `PineconeClientTest.java` — unit test with mocked HTTP responses   - `EmbeddingServiceTest.java` — unit test verifying request format and response parsing   - `ChunkBuilderTest.java` — unit test for each entity type's chunk text + metadata   - `SemanticQueryServiceTest.java` — unit test mocking Pinecone + LLM calls   - `InsightGenerationServiceTest.java` — unit test mocking data gathering + Pinecone + LLM   Frontend:   - `InsightPanel.test.tsx` — renders insight cards, handles empty/loading/error states   - `SemanticSearchInput.test.tsx` — input, submit, loading, answer display   - `QueryAnswerCard.test.tsx` — renders answer with sources and feedback buttons   ## Important constraints   - All AI features degrade gracefully. If Pinecone is down or the API key is blank, return `aiAvailable: false` and don't break the core workflow.   - Use the existing `AiSuggestionService.storeSuggestion()` to persist every AI interaction for the feedback loop.   - Use the existing `AiFeedbackButtons` component for thumbs up/down on all AI surfaces.   - The test application.yml sets `ai.provider: stub` and `ai.pinecone.api-key: ""` — tests should mock the Pinecone and embedding calls, never hit real APIs.   - Run `./gradlew test` and `npx vitest run` after each major step to verify nothing breaks. Currently: 472 backend tests, 606 frontend tests — all passing.   ## Gate checks for duet   - `cd backend && ./gradlew test` — must pass   - `cd frontend && npx vitest run` — must pass   - `cd frontend && npx tsc --noEmit` — no new errors (ignore pre-existing test file errors in Flows.test, MyWeekPage.test, ReconcilePage.test)

plan.goal: Build a RAG-powered AI insight agent for the Weekly Commit application. This includes Pinecone vector DB integration, OpenRouter embedding service, semantic search/query pipeline, proactive insight generation, event-driven indexing hooks, three new API endpoints, and frontend components (InsightPanel, SemanticSearchInput, QueryAnswerCard) wired into MyWeek and TeamWeek routes.

steps.title: Add @EnableAsync and pinecone config to application.yml + test application.yml
steps.description:
1. Add `@EnableAsync` to `Application.java` (alongside existing `@EnableScheduling`).
2. Add `ai.pinecone.api-key`, `ai.pinecone.index-name`, `ai.pinecone.environment`, and `ai.embedding.model`/`ai.embedding.dimensions` entries to `backend/src/test/resources/application.yml` (with blank api-key so tests never hit real APIs).
3. Verify that the main `application.yml` already has these entries (it does — just confirm they're complete).
4. Create Flyway migration `V10__add_ai_suggestion_team_week.sql` to add `team_id UUID REFERENCES team(id)` and `week_start_date DATE` columns to the `ai_suggestion` table. Add index: `CREATE INDEX idx_ai_suggestion_team_week ON ai_suggestion (team_id, week_start_date, suggestion_type)`. This is needed because TEAM_INSIGHT and PERSONAL_INSIGHT rows must be queryable by team+week.
5. Update `AiSuggestion.java` entity to add `teamId` (UUID, column `team_id`) and `weekStartDate` (LocalDate, column `week_start_date`) fields with getters/setters.
6. Update `AiSuggestionService.storeSuggestion()` to accept two new optional parameters (`UUID teamId`, `LocalDate weekStartDate`) via an overloaded method. The new overload calls through to the existing one then sets the two new fields before save. This avoids breaking existing callers.
7. Add new query methods to `AiSuggestionRepository`: `List<AiSuggestion> findByTeamIdAndWeekStartDateAndSuggestionType(UUID teamId, LocalDate weekStartDate, String suggestionType)` and `List<AiSuggestion> findByPlanIdAndSuggestionType(UUID planId, String suggestionType)` (already exists).

steps.title: Create EmbeddingService.java + EmbeddingServiceTest.java
steps.description:
Create `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/EmbeddingService.java`:
- Spring `@Service` with constructor injecting `@Value("${ai.openrouter.api-key:}")` apiKey, `@Value("${ai.openrouter.base-url:https://openrouter.ai/api/v1}")` baseUrl, `@Value("${ai.embedding.model:openai/text-embedding-3-small}")` model, and `ObjectMapper`.
- Uses `java.net.http.HttpClient` (same pattern as `OpenRouterAiProvider`).
- Public method `float[] embed(String text)` that POSTs to `{baseUrl}/embeddings` with body `{model, input: [text]}`, parses the response JSON to extract the `data[0].embedding` array as `float[]`.
- Public method `boolean isAvailable()` that returns false when apiKey is blank.
- All errors caught and logged; returns empty `float[0]` on failure (graceful degradation).

Create `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/EmbeddingServiceTest.java`:
- Unit test (Mockito, no Spring context needed since we test the class directly).
- Mock the HttpClient to return a canned JSON response with a 1536-dim vector.
- Verify request format (correct URL, headers, body JSON structure).
- Test blank API key returns empty array.
- Test HTTP error returns empty array.
- Follow existing test patterns from `OpenRouterAiProviderTest.java` — plain JUnit 5 + AssertJ.

steps.title: Create PineconeClient.java + PineconeClientTest.java
steps.description:
Create `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/PineconeClient.java`:
- Spring `@Service` with `@Value("${ai.pinecone.api-key:}")`, `@Value("${ai.pinecone.index-name:weekly-commit}")`, `@Value("${ai.pinecone.environment:us-east-1}")` injected.
- Uses `java.net.http.HttpClient` and `ObjectMapper`.
- `boolean isAvailable()` — returns false when api-key is blank.
- `void ensureIndexExists()` — Called lazily on first use. Calls Pinecone control plane `GET /indexes/{indexName}`. If 404, calls `POST /indexes` to create serverless index (dimension: 1536, metric: dotproduct, spec: serverless in us-east-1). Stores the resolved index host URL.
- `void upsert(String namespace, List<PineconeVector> vectors)` — POST to `{indexHost}/vectors/upsert`.
- `List<PineconeMatch> query(String namespace, float[] vector, int topK, Map<String, Object> filter)` — POST to `{indexHost}/vectors/query`.
- `void deleteByIds(String namespace, List<String> ids)` — POST to `{indexHost}/vectors/delete`.
- Inner records: `PineconeVector(String id, float[] values, Map<String, Object> metadata)`, `PineconeMatch(String id, double score, Map<String, Object> metadata)`.
- All methods gracefully handle errors (catch, log, return empty results).

Create `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/PineconeClientTest.java`:
- Unit test with mocked HttpClient/HttpResponse.
- Test upsert builds correct JSON body.
- Test query parses response correctly.
- Test blank API key makes all methods no-ops.
- Test HTTP errors return gracefully.

steps.title: Create ChunkBuilder.java + ChunkBuilderTest.java
steps.description:
Create `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/ChunkBuilder.java`:
- Spring `@Component`.
- Public methods that accept domain entities and return `ChunkData` records:
  - `ChunkData buildCommitChunk(WeeklyCommit commit, WeeklyPlan plan)` — text: `"{title}" — {chessPiece}, {estimatePoints}pts. Description: {description}. Success criteria: {successCriteria}. Outcome: {outcome}. Notes: {outcomeNotes}`. Metadata: userId=ownerUserId, teamId=plan.teamId, planId, weekStartDate=plan.weekStartDate, entityType="commit", entityId=commit.id.
  - `ChunkData buildScopeChangeChunk(ScopeChangeEvent event, WeeklyCommit commit)` — text format per spec. Metadata with entityType="scope_change".
  - `ChunkData buildCarryForwardChunk(CarryForwardLink link, WeeklyCommit sourceCommit)` — text includes reason, reasonNotes, commit's carryForwardStreak. entityType="carry_forward".
  - `ChunkData buildManagerCommentChunk(ManagerComment comment, WeeklyPlan plan)` — entityType="manager_comment", weekStartDate from plan.
  - `ChunkData buildWorkItemChunk(WorkItem item)` — entityType="ticket".
  - `ChunkData buildPlanSummaryChunk(WeeklyPlan plan, List<WeeklyCommit> commits)` — entityType="plan_summary", text: "Week of {weekStartDate}: {count} commits, {totalPoints}pts, {state}".
- Inner record `ChunkData(String id, String text, Map<String, String> metadata)` where id = `{entityType}:{entityId}`.

Create `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/ChunkBuilderTest.java`:
- Unit test for each entity type.
- Verify chunk text format and metadata keys/values.
- Test null-safe handling (null description, null outcomeNotes, etc.).

steps.title: Create SemanticIndexService.java + event-driven indexing hooks
steps.description:
Create `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/SemanticIndexService.java`:
- Spring `@Service`.
- Constructor injects `PineconeClient`, `EmbeddingService`, `ChunkBuilder`, and relevant repositories (`WeeklyPlanRepository`, `WeeklyCommitRepository`, etc.).
- `@Async public void indexEntity(String entityType, UUID entityId)` — Loads the entity from the appropriate repository, calls ChunkBuilder to build chunk, calls EmbeddingService to embed, calls PineconeClient to upsert (namespace = org ID looked up via team → org). Catches all exceptions (graceful degradation).
- `@Async public void deleteEntity(String entityType, UUID entityId)` — Calls PineconeClient.deleteByIds.
- `@Scheduled(cron = "0 0 3 * * *") public void dailySweepReindex()` — For plans updated in the last 48 hours, re-index all their commits, scope changes, carry-forwards. This catches anything that failed during async indexing. Guarded by `PineconeClient.isAvailable()`.
- All methods check `PineconeClient.isAvailable()` before doing work.

Add indexing hooks (direct calls at the end of service methods, not Spring Events as specified):
- `CommitService.java`: after `createCommit()` and `updateCommit()`, call `semanticIndexService.indexEntity("commit", savedCommit.getId())`. Inject SemanticIndexService via constructor (optional dependency — use `@Autowired(required=false)` or just inject and null-check).
- `ScopeChangeService.java`: after creating a ScopeChangeEvent, call indexEntity("scope_change", event.getId()).
- `CarryForwardService.java`: after creating a CarryForwardLink, call indexEntity("carry_forward", link.getId()).
- `ManagerReviewService.java`: after creating a ManagerComment, call indexEntity("manager_comment", comment.getId()).
- `TicketService.java`: after create/update, call indexEntity("ticket", workItem.getId()).
- `LockService.java`: after locking a plan, call indexEntity("plan_summary", plan.getId()). Also inject `InsightGenerationService` (from step-7, but add the field + null-check now with `@Autowired(required=false)`) and after a successful lock call `insightGenerationService.generatePersonalInsightsAsync(plan.getId())` — this triggers personal insight generation when a plan is finalized. The actual `InsightGenerationService` class is created in step-7; for now, the field is null and the null-check skips the call, keeping tests green.
- `ReconciliationService.java`: after reconcile submit, call indexEntity("plan_summary", plan.getId()).

The `@Async` annotation on `indexEntity()` ensures these calls don't block the write path. Since `@EnableAsync` was added in step-1, Spring will proxy the calls.

No separate test file needed for SemanticIndexService in this step — it will be indirectly tested. But ensure existing tests pass since we're modifying service constructors (use `@Autowired(required=false)` or default null for the new SemanticIndexService dependency so existing mocked tests don't break).

steps.title: Create SemanticQueryService.java + SemanticQueryServiceTest.java
steps.description:
Create `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/SemanticQueryService.java`:
- Spring `@Service`.
- Constructor injects `PineconeClient`, `EmbeddingService`, `AiProviderRegistry`, `AiSuggestionService`, `ObjectMapper`.
- Public method `RagQueryResult query(String question, UUID teamId, UUID userId)`:
  1. Check PineconeClient.isAvailable() and EmbeddingService.isAvailable(); if either is false, return unavailable result.
  2. Intent classification: call AiProviderRegistry.generateSuggestion() with an AiContext of type "RAG_INTENT" containing the question. Parse the LLM response to extract `{ intent, userFilter, timeRange, entityTypes, keywords }`. If parsing fails, use defaults (no filters, all entity types).
  3. Embed the question via EmbeddingService.embed(question).
  4. Build Pinecone metadata filter from intent (e.g., filter by teamId, entityTypes, date range).
  5. Query Pinecone: `pineconeClient.query(namespace, vector, topK=5, filter)`.
  6. Pack retrieved chunks + original question into a new AiContext (type "RAG_QUERY") and call AiProviderRegistry.generateSuggestion().
  7. Parse LLM response into structured answer: `{ answer, sources: [{entityType, entityId, weekStartDate, snippet}], confidence }`.
  8. Store via AiSuggestionService.storeSuggestion() with type "RAG_QUERY".
  9. Return RagQueryResult.
- Add "RAG_INTENT" and "RAG_QUERY" type constants to AiContext.
- Inner record `RagQueryResult(boolean available, String answer, List<RagSource> sources, double confidence, UUID suggestionId)`.
- Inner record `RagSource(String entityType, String entityId, String weekStartDate, String snippet)`.

Create prompt templates:
- `backend/src/main/resources/prompts/rag-intent.txt` — System prompt for intent classification.
- `backend/src/main/resources/prompts/rag-query.txt` — System prompt for RAG answer generation.

Update `StubAiProvider.java` to handle the new "RAG_INTENT" and "RAG_QUERY" suggestion types with canned responses.

Create `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/SemanticQueryServiceTest.java`:
- Unit test with mocked PineconeClient, EmbeddingService, AiProviderRegistry, AiSuggestionService.
- Test successful query pipeline (mock each step to return valid data).
- Test Pinecone unavailable returns unavailable result.
- Test embedding failure returns unavailable.
- Test LLM failure at intent step still proceeds with defaults.
- Test result is stored via AiSuggestionService.

steps.title: Create InsightGenerationService.java + InsightGenerationServiceTest.java
steps.description:
Create `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/InsightGenerationService.java`:
- Spring `@Service`.
- Constructor injects `PineconeClient`, `EmbeddingService`, `AiProviderRegistry`, `AiSuggestionService`, `TeamRepository`, `TeamMembershipRepository`, `WeeklyPlanRepository`, `WeeklyCommitRepository`, `CarryForwardLinkRepository`, `ScopeChangeEventRepository`, `ObjectMapper`.
- `@Scheduled(cron = "0 0 8 * * *") public void generateDailyInsights()` — Iterates all teams. For each, calls `generateTeamInsights(teamId, currentWeekStartDate)`.
- Public method `void generateTeamInsights(UUID teamId, LocalDate weekStartDate)`:
  1. Check PineconeClient + EmbeddingService availability.
  2. Gather current week data: plans for team members, their commits, scope changes, carry-forward links.
  3. Build a summary context string.
  4. Embed a query like "patterns in this team's last 4 weeks" and query Pinecone for historical context.
  5. Send to AiProviderRegistry with type "TEAM_INSIGHT" — system prompt asks for 3-5 actionable management insights, each citing specific plans/commits/tickets.
  6. Parse structured response: `{ insights: [{ insightText, severity, sourceEntityIds, actionSuggestion }] }`.
  7. Store each insight via the new `AiSuggestionService.storeSuggestion()` overload (from step-1), passing `teamId=teamId` and `weekStartDate=weekStartDate`. Set `suggestion_type = 'TEAM_INSIGHT'`, `planId = null`, `userId = null`. This persists the team+week identity directly in the `ai_suggestion` row so the read endpoint can query `findByTeamIdAndWeekStartDateAndSuggestionType(teamId, weekStartDate, "TEAM_INSIGHT")`.
- Public method `void generatePersonalInsights(UUID planId)` — Loads the plan, gathers its commits/scope-changes/carry-forwards. Embeds a query like "patterns for this user's recent plans" and queries Pinecone for the user's historical context (filter by userId). Sends to LLM with type "PERSONAL_INSIGHT". Stores each insight via `AiSuggestionService.storeSuggestion()` with `planId=planId`, `userId=plan.ownerUserId`, `teamId=plan.teamId`, `weekStartDate=plan.weekStartDate`, and `suggestion_type='PERSONAL_INSIGHT'`. This is called from two places:
  1. **On plan lock** — `LockService.lockPlan()` calls `insightGenerationService.generatePersonalInsightsAsync(planId)` (hook added in step-5). The `@Async` annotation ensures it doesn't block the lock transaction.
  2. **Daily scheduled job** — `generateDailyInsights()` iterates all teams, generates team insights, and also generates personal insights for each LOCKED plan in the current week that doesn't already have PERSONAL_INSIGHT rows (idempotency check via `findByPlanIdAndSuggestionType(planId, "PERSONAL_INSIGHT")`). This catches plans that were locked while Pinecone/LLM was unavailable.
- `@Async public void generatePersonalInsightsAsync(UUID planId)` — Thin async wrapper that calls `generatePersonalInsights(planId)` inside a try/catch (graceful degradation). This is the method LockService calls.
- Add "TEAM_INSIGHT" and "PERSONAL_INSIGHT" type constants to AiContext.

Create prompt templates:
- `backend/src/main/resources/prompts/team-insight.txt`
- `backend/src/main/resources/prompts/personal-insight.txt`

Update `StubAiProvider.java` to handle TEAM_INSIGHT and PERSONAL_INSIGHT with canned responses.

Create `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/InsightGenerationServiceTest.java`:
- Unit test with mocked dependencies.
- Test team insight generation stores correct number of suggestions with teamId and weekStartDate set.
- Test personal insight generation stores with planId, userId, teamId, weekStartDate set.
- Test personal insight idempotency (skips if suggestions already exist for the plan).
- Test unavailable Pinecone skips gracefully.
- Test scheduled job iterates teams and generates both team + personal insights.

steps.title: Add RAG DTOs and new AiController endpoints
steps.description:
Create DTOs in `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/dto/`:
- `RagQueryRequest.java` — record with `String question`, `UUID teamId` (optional), `UUID userId` (optional). Add `@NotBlank` on question.
- `RagQueryResponse.java` — record with `boolean aiAvailable`, `String answer`, `List<RagSourceDto> sources`, `double confidence`, `UUID suggestionId`.
- `RagSourceDto.java` — record with `String entityType`, `String entityId`, `String weekStartDate`, `String snippet`.
- `InsightCardDto.java` — record with `UUID suggestionId`, `String insightText`, `String severity`, `List<String> sourceEntityIds`, `String actionSuggestion`, `String createdAt`.
- `InsightListResponse.java` — record with `boolean aiAvailable`, `List<InsightCardDto> insights`.

The repository query methods added in step-1 (`findByTeamIdAndWeekStartDateAndSuggestionType` and the existing `findByPlanIdAndSuggestionType`) provide the data layer for these endpoints.

Add to `AiController.java`:
- `@PostMapping("/api/ai/query")` — Validates request, calls `SemanticQueryService.query()`, maps to `RagQueryResponse`. Graceful degradation: if service returns unavailable, return `{aiAvailable: false}`.
- `@GetMapping("/api/teams/{id}/week/{weekStart}/ai-insights")` — Calls `AiSuggestionRepository.findByTeamIdAndWeekStartDateAndSuggestionType(teamId, weekStart, "TEAM_INSIGHT")`. Parses each suggestion's `suggestionPayload` JSON into `InsightCardDto`. Returns `InsightListResponse`. Because step-1 added `team_id` and `week_start_date` columns and step-7 stores them on every TEAM_INSIGHT row, this query reliably returns the correct records.
- `@GetMapping("/api/plans/{id}/ai-insights")` — Calls `AiSuggestionRepository.findByPlanIdAndSuggestionType(planId, "PERSONAL_INSIGHT")`. Parses payload into `InsightCardDto`. Returns `InsightListResponse`.

Import and inject `SemanticQueryService` and `AiSuggestionRepository` into AiController constructor.

Ensure all endpoints follow the existing graceful degradation pattern (try/catch wrapping, returning aiAvailable: false on any exception).

steps.title: Create frontend API client and hooks for RAG endpoints
steps.description:
Create `frontend/src/api/ragApi.ts`:
- Follow the exact pattern of `aiApi.ts` — export types and a `createRagApi(client, actorUserId)` factory.
- Types: `RagQueryRequest { question: string; teamId?: string; userId?: string }`, `RagQueryResponse { aiAvailable: boolean; answer?: string; sources?: RagSource[]; confidence?: number; suggestionId?: string }`, `RagSource { entityType: string; entityId: string; weekStartDate?: string; snippet?: string }`, `InsightCard { suggestionId: string; insightText: string; severity: string; sourceEntityIds: string[]; actionSuggestion: string; createdAt: string }`, `InsightListResponse { aiAvailable: boolean; insights: InsightCard[] }`.
- API methods: `submitQuery(req)` → POST /ai/query, `getTeamInsights(teamId, weekStart)` → GET /teams/{id}/week/{weekStart}/ai-insights, `getPlanInsights(planId)` → GET /plans/{id}/ai-insights.

Create `frontend/src/api/ragHooks.ts`:
- Follow the pattern of `aiHooks.ts`.
- `useRagApi()` — creates a stable RagApi instance.
- `useTeamInsights(teamId: string | null, weekStart: string)` — uses `useQuery` to fetch team insights.
- `usePlanInsights(planId: string | null)` — uses `useQuery` to fetch plan insights.
- `useSemanticQuery()` — returns `{ mutate, data, loading, error }` pattern for the POST mutation. Implement as a custom hook with useState for loading/error/data and a `mutate(question, teamId?, userId?)` function.

steps.title: Create QueryAnswerCard.tsx + QueryAnswerCard.test.tsx
steps.description:
Create `frontend/src/components/ai/QueryAnswerCard.tsx`:
- Props: `answer: string`, `sources: RagSource[]`, `confidence: number`, `suggestionId: string`.
- Renders answer text in a Card component.
- Renders source citations as a list with entity type badges (using Badge component) and clickable links. For 'commit' entities, link to plan. For 'ticket' entities, link to ticket page. For others, show entity type + week date.
- Confidence indicator: color-coded bar or text (green ≥ 0.8, yellow ≥ 0.5, red < 0.5).
- Includes `AiFeedbackButtons` component with the `suggestionId`.
- Follow existing component patterns (lucide icons, cn() utility, data-testid attributes).

Create `frontend/src/__tests__/QueryAnswerCard.test.tsx`:
- Mock `useAiApi` and `useHostBridge` (same pattern as other AI component tests).
- Test renders answer text.
- Test renders sources with entity type badges.
- Test renders confidence indicator.
- Test renders AiFeedbackButtons.
- Test handles empty sources array.

steps.title: Create SemanticSearchInput.tsx + SemanticSearchInput.test.tsx
steps.description:
Create `frontend/src/components/ai/SemanticSearchInput.tsx`:
- Props: `teamId?: string`, `userId?: string`.
- Contains a text input and submit button.
- On submit, calls `useSemanticQuery().mutate(question, teamId, userId)`.
- Shows loading state while query runs (~2s): spinner/pulse animation with text "Searching...".
- When data arrives, renders `QueryAnswerCard` below the input.
- Error state shows error message with retry option.
- Uses lucide `Search` icon, `Bot` icon for AI branding.
- data-testid attributes: `semantic-search-input`, `semantic-search-submit`, `semantic-search-loading`, `semantic-search-error`.

Create `frontend/src/__tests__/SemanticSearchInput.test.tsx`:
- Mock `ragHooks.ts` (useSemanticQuery).
- Test renders input and submit button.
- Test submit triggers mutation.
- Test loading state renders.
- Test answer display renders QueryAnswerCard.
- Test error state renders.

steps.title: Create InsightPanel.tsx + InsightPanel.test.tsx
steps.description:
Create `frontend/src/components/ai/InsightPanel.tsx`:
- Props: `mode: 'team' | 'personal'`, `teamId?: string`, `weekStart?: string`, `planId?: string`.
- If mode='team', uses `useTeamInsights(teamId, weekStart)`. If mode='personal', uses `usePlanInsights(planId)`.
- Renders a list of insight cards. Each card shows:
  - Severity indicator (color-coded badge: 'high' = red, 'medium' = amber, 'low' = blue).
  - Insight text.
  - Action suggestion (italicized, lighter text).
  - Source entity links (clickable).
  - AiFeedbackButtons with the suggestion's ID.
- Loading state: skeleton cards (use Skeleton component from ui/).
- Empty state: "No AI insights available for this period."
- Error state: "Failed to load insights" message.
- aiAvailable=false state: subtle disabled message (same pattern as AiLintPanel unavailable state).
- data-testid: `insight-panel`, `insight-card-{id}`, `insight-panel-loading`, `insight-panel-empty`, `insight-panel-unavailable`.

Create `frontend/src/__tests__/InsightPanel.test.tsx`:
- Mock `ragHooks.ts`.
- Test renders insight cards for team mode.
- Test renders insight cards for personal mode.
- Test loading state.
- Test empty state.
- Test error state.
- Test aiAvailable=false state.
- Test AiFeedbackButtons rendered per card.

steps.title: Wire InsightPanel and SemanticSearchInput into MyWeek.tsx and TeamWeek.tsx
steps.description:
Edit `frontend/src/routes/MyWeek.tsx`:
- Import `InsightPanel` from `../components/ai/InsightPanel.js`.
- Add `<InsightPanel mode="personal" planId={plan?.id} />` below the plan header Card and above the CapacityMeter, conditionally rendered when `plan` is defined.

Edit `frontend/src/routes/TeamWeek.tsx`:
- Import `InsightPanel` from `../components/ai/InsightPanel.js`.
- Import `SemanticSearchInput` from `../components/ai/SemanticSearchInput.js`.
- Add `SemanticSearchInput` in the page header area (after the team name span, inside the header div).
- Add `InsightPanel` as a collapsible section above the tab navigation bar. Use a `useState` for collapse state with a toggle button. Default to collapsed. Render `<InsightPanel mode="team" teamId={teamId} weekStart={weekStartDate} />` inside the collapsible section.

Both additions should be guarded by the `aiAssistanceEnabled` feature flag from `bridge.context.featureFlags` to avoid rendering when AI is disabled.

Verify existing tests still pass — the key risk is that existing TeamWeekPage.test.tsx and MyWeekPage.test.tsx mock specific hooks. Since InsightPanel uses `ragHooks` (not already mocked in those tests), we need to add a vi.mock for `ragHooks.ts` returning default loading/empty state in both test files, or ensure the InsightPanel gracefully handles the unmocked state. The safest approach: add `vi.mock('../api/ragHooks.js', ...)` to the existing test files returning `{ useTeamInsights: vi.fn(() => ({ data: undefined, loading: false, error: null, refetch: vi.fn() })), usePlanInsights: vi.fn(() => ({ data: undefined, loading: false, error: null, refetch: vi.fn() })), useSemanticQuery: vi.fn(() => ({ mutate: vi.fn(), data: null, loading: false, error: null })), useRagApi: vi.fn() }`.
